steps.goal: Rephrase the provided facts into concise, clear statements suitable for inclusion in an operator-style note. The facts are: (1) Open-Eywa treats the node as the durable unit of work. (2) Mission runs are stored in mission folders. (3) Testing improves reliability.
steps.title: Rephrase facts for note clarity

steps.goal: Write the first part of a short operator-style note, focusing on Open-Eywa's treatment of the node as the durable unit of work. This part should be based solely on the rephrased fact from Step 1.
steps.title: Draft Part 1 of the note: Node as durable unit

steps.goal: Write the second part of a short operator-style note, focusing on mission runs being stored in mission folders. This part should be based solely on the rephrased fact from Step 1.
steps.title: Draft Part 2 of the note: Mission run storage

steps.goal: Write the third part of a short operator-style note, focusing on how testing improves reliability. This part should be based solely on the rephrased fact from Step 1.
steps.title: Draft Part 3 of the note: Importance of testing

steps.goal: Combine the three drafted parts from Steps 2, 3, and 4 into a cohesive, short operator-style note. Ensure the final note is presented in three distinct parts and uses only the facts provided in the original prompt.
steps.title: Synthesize the final operator-style note
